title: LG unveils 'world's first' native 1000 Hz refresh rate at 1080p for serious competitive gaming UltraGear 25G590B to launch in the second half of 2026
summary: LG unveiled the world's first native 1000Hz refresh rate monitor, the UltraGear 25G590B, supporting 1080p resolution. Unlike previous dual-mode displays, this monitor maintains full-HD resolution for immersive gaming. It is set to launch in the second half of 2026.
insight: Higher refresh rates enhance competitive gaming experience and immersion.

title: Intel's Project Firefly creates sub-$600 laptops to compete with Apple's MacBook Neo leverages China's smartphone manufacturing blueprint to produce affordable Wildcat Lake systems
summary: Intel's Project Firefly uses China's smartphone blueprint to create affordable laptops, leveraging Wildcat Lake processors.
insight: Disrupting traditional laptop pricing with innovative manufacturing strategies.

title: Researchers attack AMD's Infinity Fabric to bypass hardware security protections with 'Fabricked' flaw lets malicious cloud hosts silently read confidential VM memory and forge attestation reports
summary: ETH Zurich researchers found a software-only attack on AMD Infinity Fabric, allowing malicious cloud hosts to read confidential VM memory and forge attestation reports on EPYC systems.
insight: AMD Infinity Fabric bypassed, compromising sensitive cloud data security

title: Custom-built passive water-cooled PC relies on convection chimney effect powered by triple-stacked radiators 9800X3D build with RTX 5080 has no fans, copper, and radiator chimney, but struggles to keep temperatures down
summary: Billet Labs' custom-built passive PC relies on a convection chimney effect powered by triple-stacked radiators, achieving a fanless gaming build. This innovative cooling system utilizes copper and a radiator chimney to dissipate heat. The build features a powerful AMD Ryzen 9 9800X3D and NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080.
insight: Passive cooling systems push thermal limits with innovative design approaches.

title: Fans secretly mapped 24 terabytes of Minecraft s infamous 2b2t server, million square block archive to be shared via torrent Intense digital archaeology project has taken years, risked player wrath
summary: A massive digital archaeology project has archived 24TB of 2b2t server data, preserving a million squared blocks of Minecraft history.
insight: Preserving online gaming history for future generations and research purposes.

title: Next-gen mobile gaming just got $1,150 cheaper Dell slashes RTX 5090 gaming laptop price tag to $4,399
summary: Dell has temporarily reduced the price of its Alienware 16 Area-51 gaming laptop with GeForce RTX 5090 to $4,399.99, saving customers $1,150.
insight: High-end gaming laptops become more affordable for consumers.

title: Jury throws out Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI after less than two hours of deliberation Unanimous vote that Musk filed the lawsuit too late
summary: A federal jury in California rejected Elon Musk's lawsuit against OpenAI, citing a late filing. The unanimous decision was made after less than two hours of deliberation. Musk's claims were dismissed in their entirety.
insight: Timing can be a crucial factor in high-stakes business lawsuits.

title: Record-high pricing pushes SSD and memory makers to borrow $880 million just to afford buying chips Adata, TeamGroup, and others take on substantial debt to survive shortages
summary: SSD and memory makers borrow $880 million to afford chip shortages, with Adata taking on the most debt.
insight: Chip shortages force manufacturers to take on substantial debt to survive.

title: Why now is the best time to jump on the OLED monitor bandwagon breaking down new-gen panel tech and our top burn-in prevention tips
summary: OLED monitors are becoming a more viable option due to improved reliability and decreasing prices, making them an attractive choice for those seeking high-quality visuals.
insight: Advancements in OLED tech make it a viable mainstream alternative now.

title: China says 'world's first' offshore wind-powered underwater data center has entered full operation, houses 2,000 servers 24 megawatt subsea AI facility uses ocean water for passive cooling and offshore wind for power
summary: China has launched a 24 MW underwater AI data center off Shanghai's coast, using offshore wind power and seawater cooling to reduce energy consumption and improve efficiency.
insight: Pioneering sustainable data storage with reduced energy consumption.

title: Linus Torvalds says flood of duplicate AI-generated vulnerability reports have made Linux security mailing list 'almost entirely unmanageable' private list 'a waste of time for everybody involved' in switch to new public system
summary: Linus Torvalds criticizes private AI-generated vulnerability reports on Linux mailing list, saying they're a waste of time due to being publicly detectable.
insight: Publicly detectable AI bugs make private reporting redundant and ineffective.

title: Ubuntu Core 26 targets IoT devices and embedded systems, offers up to 15 years of security maintenance
summary: Canonical has released Ubuntu Core 26, a minimal operating system designed for IoT devices and embedded systems, offering up to 15 years of security maintenance and optimized OTA updates.
insight: Enhanced security for IoT devices meets EU Cyber Resilience Act requirements.

title: $60 kit transforms the Raspberry Pi 4/5 into a DIN Rail industrial computer with isolated RS232, RS485, and CAN Bus
summary: The Waveshare kit transforms Raspberry Pi 4/5 into an industrial computer with isolated interfaces, offering a compact DIN rail or wall mount solution for industrial applications.
insight: Industrial-grade computing made accessible to Raspberry Pi users inexpensively.

title: ESP32-S3 PowerFeather V2 board gains support for LiFePO4/LFP batteries
summary: The ESP32-S3 PowerFeather V2 board now supports LiFePO4/LFP batteries, along with Li-Ion/LiPo. It features an Analog Devices fuel gauge and TPS631013 buck-boost regulator to ensure stable 3.3V power. This board is ideal for IoT applications with solar panel input and various battery options.
insight: Enhanced battery safety and longer lifespan for IoT applications.

title: The Best Mouse: Our Favorite Mice for Work and Play
summary: Discover top-rated mice for work, gaming, and everyday use, covering budget to flagship models.
insight: Ergonomic design and precision matter for productivity and gaming performance alike.

title: Elon Musk loses OpenAI trial after jury says he waited too long to sue
summary: Elon Musk loses OpenAI trial for waiting too long to sue.
insight: Musk's delayed lawsuit jeopardizes his claims against OpenAI's actions.

title: Amazon's Alexa can now generate podcasts on whatever topic you want. But, will anyone listen?
summary: Amazon's Alexa can now create podcasts on any topic, partnering with reputable sources for accurate information.
insight: AI-generated content may revolutionize podcasting, accessibility, and information dissemination.

title: TP-Link's tiny 5-port Gigabit switch drops to just $9
summary: The TP-Link LiteWave LS1005G is a 5-port Gigabit switch available for $9, a 50% discount from its usual Amazon price.
insight: Affordable high-speed networking for home and small business setups

title: ArXiv will ban researchers for a year if they submit papers with AI slop
summary: ArXiv updates code of conduct to deter AI-generated 'slop', enforcing a one-year ban for violators.
insight: Authenticity of research is at stake with AI-generated content proliferating.

title: Terraria celebrates 15 years of crafting, mining, and survival
summary: Terraria celebrates 15 years of crafting, mining, and survival gameplay. The game can be played alone or online with others, and it has been ported to various platforms. Its engaging experience is highlighted by a great soundtrack.
insight: Long-running indie game series maintains enduring community appeal.

title: Researchers unveil NAND-DRAM hybrid that could outperform traditional memory
summary: Imec has developed a 3D NAND-DRAM hybrid memory technology, combining DRAM speed and NAND storage density.
insight: Overcoming the memory wall bottleneck for AI applications

title: One company now controls more than 4% of all Bitcoin
summary: A company now controls over 4% of all Bitcoin, with holdings valued at $64.6 billion. This follows a recent spending spree of just over $2 billion. The current price of Bitcoin is around $76,574 per unit.
insight: Concentration of wealth and control in the Bitcoin market increases

title: Dell makes its strongest case yet for keeping AI out of the cloud
summary: Dell is showcasing customer deployments and new products that demonstrate AI workloads can be run on-premises, challenging the cloud-only AI narrative.
insight: On-premises AI infrastructure offers control and security advantages for enterprises.

title: AMD Radeon RX 9070 XT deal drops to $629 as GPU prices slowly cool off
summary: The Radeon RX 9070 XT Gaming OC by Gigabyte has dropped to $629 on Amazon, marking a lower price point for the GPU. This deal features 16GB GDDR6 memory, triple-fan cooling, factory overclocking, and suitable performance for 1440p gaming and 4K titles. The Lightning Deal highlights a trend of cooling GPU prices.
insight: GPU prices slowly decline, making high-end options more accessible.

title: A Windows 11 speed boost is coming as soon as next month, along with a taskbar you can finally move
summary: Windows 11 is set to receive a speed boost and taskbar updates next month, addressing user skepticism and performance concerns.
insight: Microsoft prioritizes performance to win back disillusioned Windows users.

title: Discord improves Linux support with Steam Deck and distro-friendly upgrades
summary: Discord has improved its Linux support with official packages for Fedora and Arch distros, a self-updater, and hardware video acceleration for various GPUs, bringing the experience closer to Windows.
insight: Enhanced Linux support expands Discord's user base and accessibility.

title: Forget Raspberry Pi: this computer is the literally the size of a credit card
summary: Raspberry Pi boards are not truly credit card-sized due to thickness and ports, misleading their description.
insight: Raspberry Pi boards are not credit card thin or practical.

title: The first computer mouse: how many buttons did it have?
summary: The first computer mouse, developed by Douglas Engelbart and his team, had one button. Released in 1964, it revolutionized human-computer interaction. The single button facilitated basic navigation and selection functions.
insight: Single-button mouse simplicity laid foundational UI navigation principles.

title: VMware Workstation Pro 26H1 brings broader Linux support and quality-of-life upgrades
summary: VMware Workstation Pro 26H1 adds broader Linux support and quality-of-life upgrades.
insight: Enhanced virtualization for developers and tinkerers with Linux and Windows.

title: Japan is using $4,000 animatronic wolves to scare off bears, and can't make them fast enough
summary: Japan is using $4,000 animatronic wolves to deter bears due to a surge in attacks and sightings.
insight: Bear attacks pose significant threat to human safety and Japanese wildlife.
